title: Mliečna Pólaš
description: A traditional Slovakian fermented crumbly sauerkraut hash dish, infused with the sweetness of redcurrants and the crunch of toast, all in a paleo-friendly one-pot meal.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the shredded cabbage and caraway seeds. Massage the cabbage with your hands for 5 minutes to help release its juices.
In a large cast-iron skillet or Dutch oven, heat the coconut oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is translucent and starting to caramelize (about 8-10 minutes).
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the shredded cabbage mixture to the skillet, and stir to combine with the onion and garlic.
In a small bowl, whisk together the redcurrant juice, apple cider vinegar, water, salt, and black pepper. Pour the mixture over the cabbage mixture, and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
During the last 10 minutes of cooking, stir in the crumbled toasted rye bread.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Using a slotted spoon, transfer the sauerkraut hash to a serving dish. Serve hot, garnished with additional toasted rye bread crumbs and redcurrants, if desired.
